steps.title: Translate the cruising speed range to inequalities
steps.content:
The speed varied between 150 and 170 means the speed must satisfy:
  • At least 150 mph: $s \geq 150$
  • At most 170 mph: $s \leq 170$
Combining both conditions: $$150 \leq s \leq 170$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $s \leq 20$ — Way too small for an airplane cruising speed.
  • Choice B: $s \leq 150$ — Only provides an upper limit; ignores that speeds go up to 170.
  • Choice C: $s \leq 170$ — Only provides an upper limit; ignores the lower bound of 150.
  • Choice D: $150 \leq s \leq 170$ — Exactly matches the compound inequality we derived.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
